expectations.text: Sets maxUnavailable: 0 in the rollingUpdate strategy and explains this prevents the replica count from dropping during rollout
expectations.evidence: Shows `maxUnavailable: 0` with comment "NEVER drop below 3 pods during rollout".

expectations.text: Defines a readinessProbe (not just a livenessProbe or minReadySeconds alone) and states that without it traffic is routed to Pods that have started but may not yet be serving
expectations.evidence: Has readinessProbe with failureThreshold: 1. Key decisions: "readinessProbe — prevents traffic to pods that haven't finished starting."

expectations.text: Sets minReadySeconds to a non-zero value (10-30s) and explains it reduces but does not eliminate the unready-traffic window
expectations.evidence: Shows `minReadySeconds: 15` with note "new pod must stay ready for 15s before old pod is terminated".

expectations.text: Includes a kubernetes.io/change-cause annotation to populate rollout history
expectations.evidence: Shows `annotations: kubernetes.io/change-cause: v1.0.0` in the Deployment metadata.

expectations.text: Warns that with maxUnavailable: 0 a rollout hangs indefinitely if the new Pod never becomes ready, and recommends progressDeadlineSeconds plus kubectl rollout undo to recover
expectations.evidence: No mention of rollout hanging, progressDeadlineSeconds, or kubectl rollout undo as recovery mechanism.

expectations.text: Sets maxUnavailable: 0 in the rollingUpdate strategy and explains this prevents the replica count from dropping during rollout
expectations.evidence: Shows `maxUnavailable: 0` with "Zero-downtime: never have fewer pods than desired during rollout".

expectations.text: Defines a readinessProbe (not just a livenessProbe or minReadySeconds alone) and states that without it traffic is routed to Pods that have started but may not yet be serving
expectations.evidence: Has readinessProbe. "Readiness probe: Traffic isn't routed until the pod passes this check. Separated from liveness because 'ready to serve' ≠ 'process alive'."

expectations.text: Sets minReadySeconds to a non-zero value (10-30s) and explains it reduces but does not eliminate the unready-traffic window
expectations.evidence: No minReadySeconds field in the Deployment manifest.

expectations.text: Includes a kubernetes.io/change-cause annotation to populate rollout history
expectations.evidence: No kubernetes.io/change-cause annotation in the Deployment metadata.

expectations.text: Warns that with maxUnavailable: 0 a rollout hangs indefinitely if the new Pod never becomes ready, and recommends progressDeadlineSeconds plus kubectl rollout undo to recover
expectations.evidence: No mention of rollout hanging, progressDeadlineSeconds, or kubectl rollout undo.

expectations.text: Recommends a Job (not a Deployment or init container) as the correct resource for a one-time task that runs to completion
expectations.evidence: "Use a Job with restartPolicy: Never." Uses Job resource.

expectations.text: Sets restartPolicy: Never (not OnFailure) and explains that Never keeps the failed Pod for log inspection while OnFailure restarts in-place and may overwrite logs
expectations.evidence: "With Never, a failed Pod stays around so you can inspect its logs. With OnFailure, the container restarts in-place and previous logs may be overwritten."

expectations.text: Sets ttlSecondsAfterFinished and explains that without it completed Jobs and their Pods accumulate until manually deleted
expectations.evidence: Shows `ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 86400`. "Without it, completed Jobs and their Pods accumulate forever."

expectations.text: Sets backoffLimit to a low value (0 or 1) appropriate for a migration, not the default of 6
expectations.evidence: Shows `backoffLimit: 1` with explanation "Migrations are often not idempotent — retrying a partially-completed migration can corrupt data."

expectations.text: Avoids recommending restartPolicy: Always, which is invalid for Jobs and only valid for Deployments
expectations.evidence: Uses restartPolicy: Never; no mention of Always.

expectations.text: Recommends a Job (not a Deployment or init container) as the correct resource for a one-time task that runs to completion
expectations.evidence: Uses Job resource. "Why Job over init container: Job pods persist after completion."

expectations.text: Sets restartPolicy: Never (not OnFailure) and explains that Never keeps the failed Pod for log inspection while OnFailure restarts in-place and may overwrite logs
expectations.evidence: "With Never, each failure creates a new pod (preserving the failed pod's logs). With OnFailure, the same pod restarts (losing previous logs)."

expectations.text: Sets ttlSecondsAfterFinished and explains that without it completed Jobs and their Pods accumulate until manually deleted
expectations.evidence: Shows `ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 86400` with "Clean up after 24 hours (keeps logs available)".

expectations.text: Sets backoffLimit to a low value (0 or 1) appropriate for a migration, not the default of 6
expectations.evidence: Shows `backoffLimit: 3` — not a low value appropriate for a migration (default is 6, but 3 is still too high for an idempotency-sensitive migration).

expectations.text: Avoids recommending restartPolicy: Always, which is invalid for Jobs and only valid for Deployments
expectations.evidence: Uses restartPolicy: Never; no mention of Always.

expectations.text: Sets concurrencyPolicy: Forbid (not Allow or Replace) and explains it skips the new Job if the previous is still running, which is correct for backups that must not overlap
expectations.evidence: Shows `concurrencyPolicy: Forbid` with "if the previous Job is still running when the next schedule fires, the new Job is simply skipped. No overlap, no corruption."

expectations.text: Provides the exact kubectl create job --from=cronjob/<name> command to trigger a Job immediately without waiting for the schedule
expectations.evidence: Shows `kubectl create job --from=cronjob/backup backup-manual-test -n db`.

expectations.text: Increases failedJobsHistoryLimit above the default of 1 (recommends 10) to retain enough failed Job logs for inspection
expectations.evidence: Shows `failedJobsHistoryLimit: 10`.

expectations.text: Distinguishes concurrencyPolicy: Replace (kills the previous Job) from Forbid (skips the new one), explaining why Replace is wrong for backups
expectations.evidence: No mention of concurrencyPolicy: Replace. Only Forbid is discussed.

expectations.text: Avoids recommending a Deployment or any resource other than CronJob for the recurring scheduled task
expectations.evidence: Uses CronJob throughout; no alternative resource recommended.

expectations.text: Sets concurrencyPolicy: Forbid (not Allow or Replace) and explains it skips the new Job if the previous is still running, which is correct for backups that must not overlap
expectations.evidence: Shows `concurrencyPolicy: Forbid` with "If the previous Job is still running when the next schedule fires, skip the new one entirely."

expectations.text: Provides the exact kubectl create job --from=cronjob/<name> command to trigger a Job immediately without waiting for the schedule
expectations.evidence: Shows `kubectl create job db-backup-manual --from=cronjob/db-backup -n production`.

expectations.text: Increases failedJobsHistoryLimit above the default of 1 (recommends 10) to retain enough failed Job logs for inspection
expectations.evidence: Shows `failedJobsHistoryLimit: 3` — above the default of 1 but not 10 as recommended.

expectations.text: Distinguishes concurrencyPolicy: Replace (kills the previous Job) from Forbid (skips the new one), explaining why Replace is wrong for backups
expectations.evidence: "Replace: Kill the running Job and start a new one. Bad for backups (corrupts in-progress backup)."

expectations.text: Avoids recommending a Deployment or any resource other than CronJob for the recurring scheduled task
expectations.evidence: Uses CronJob throughout; no alternative resource recommended.
